Main reason being that it has got ample space, it's not small as others in my area or around. Yes it's a bit far from JVTS GARDEN but not to far tho. It's in A1 block on mata road. As other mandir it also consists of many idols of different god and goddess. The main attraction is the Maa Kali idol in the middle. It may be a new addition but it's really big n different. The mandir has additional space for may be functions , festivals or bhandara for serving huge crowd. Panditji was cool n supportive. Tho this mandir doesn't have any permanent roof but still very famous among locals and visitors. It's a Must visit. Rather visit this street, it's full of hugely worship places. title: Khatu shyam mandir
address: Shyam,dhaam,mandir, 100 Feet Rd, Block A1, Chhatarpur, New Delhi, Delhi 110074
user_reviews: [{"Name":"Amit K Khurana (A K K)","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUGjDGQDxa5oYjys14ThA2eXoHSxwSHHxlClU_eVNl4-XhgPo8=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"So this Khatu Shyam mandir on 100 futa road is  extremely neglected by general public. May be only surrounding people know about this mandir. Though it's a very good mandir in my opinion. It looks new, very neat and clean. Its on roadside but as I said it's neglected because it doesn't look like mandir from outside, it's square in shape and doesn't have a domb like structure unlike most other mandir have, i mean untill one doesn't watch it closely you will pass by it, though Bhajan song n sound is very much audible, still busy person will ignore it , not intentionally. The structure looks like kind of residence or business but not mandir . But when u enter the mandir u find it hard to believe that it's beautiful. There are many idols of different god and goddesses . The marble flooring is great, the pillars look cool. It has decent space inside. Without crowd it looks more spacious. Some pictures and videos for your immediate view. Do visit at least once. Chao","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sAF1QipMqDT4PYHbDem8VAwQx2X9W9NfihqzpwyDhtXI6\u0026fid=0x0:0x1f77884aebaad769"],"When":"2023-6-7"},{"Name":"Chirag tiwari","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXsG7g4r9EuMixi1IFPGgecfRUD8Y25iT7Ti98fSMmBT5oWQtrR=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Best","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Raj Kumar","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUDVzP0f6Yzli3hO_oSnVrsPYknOOqpENJN3VD9uUlCf8LDxQhQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Awesome","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Aryan gamer gamer","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVxjlv5Y0_aqqC6PwxjyO-bJYvPPjsx1xQdO1A1lSKDN5R-nL_e=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Oo","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Sourabh Mantri","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJC66oT1ZV_LivtVCzKpa5UgFhRDiD9DDJ_YodzXzWqmVuomI4=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Chitra Gola","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIjkQvnU4w0_LPY7tricSfyZFCcRKdEU643VvWFdPtbcYuuNQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"","Images":null,"When":""}]

The premises of the temple is spread over large area. There are temple of Shree Hanuman Jee, Shree Vishnu Jee, Nine avatars of Devi, Shree Mahalaxmi jee and Shree Mahadev jee! The place is clean, well maintained and assures you of peace and inner tranquility. There's a giant Hanuman jee murthy which surely is delight for photography. This is adjacent to parking space, which is sufficient enough for even 200+ cars. During Navratri festivals (take place 9 days twice a year), the crowd is beyond usual.
Make sure you have enough time to travel, stand in queue, and get back.
Hanuman temple is also one of the special attractions of this shrine compound.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sAF1QipMqmmtNtYy2rMJKbfa-SJKULBGdNcWR0zqhnGL2&fid=0x0:0xe80caddfc811fe62"],"When":"2024-5-23"},{"Name":"Shubham Sharma","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVu5de6D8H4tiXxguGw1ht-NKv3PFgCUJru1bkBgobPOUiXXejo=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Chattarpur Mandir: A Journey of Faith and Grandeur

Chattarpur Mandir, nestled in the heart of South Delhi, isn't just a temple, it's an experience. It's a testament to devotion, a haven of peace, and a celebration of Indian architecture. Here's a peek into what makes it unique:

More than just a temple: Chattarpur Mandir is dedicated to Goddess Durga, but it also houses shrines to other deities like Shiva, Vishnu, and Ganesha. This makes it a spiritual hub for devotees seeking blessings from various forms of the Divine.

A sprawling complex: The temple complex spreads across a massive 70 acres, making it one of the largest in India. You'll be welcomed by a grand entrance, adorned with intricate carvings and sculptures, followed by a series of prayer halls, gardens, and a serene lake.

The magic of the architecture: The temple itself is a marvel of modern architecture, built with white marble and red sandstone. Its towering spires and intricate carvings evoke a sense of awe and grandeur.

A vibrant atmosphere: The temple buzzes with activity throughout the day. Devotees from all walks of life come here to offer prayers, seek solace, and participate in religious ceremonies. The air vibrates with the chants of mantras, the ringing of bells, and the gentle rustling of prayer flags.

Beyond the religious: Chattarpur Mandir is also a popular destination for visitors looking for a peaceful retreat. The serene gardens, the tranquil lake, and the overall serene atmosphere offer a welcome break from the hustle and bustle of city life.

Here's what to expect during your visit:

• Prepare for a crowd: Especially during festivals like Navratri and Durga Puja, the temple draws massive crowds. Be patient and respectful of other devotees.
• Wear modest clothing: It's customary to cover your head and shoulders while entering the temple.
• Embrace the spirituality: The energy here is palpable. Take time to reflect, offer your prayers, and soak in the atmosphere.
• Explore the complex: Don't just limit yourself to the main temple. Take time to visit the various shrines, stroll through the gardens, and admire the intricate carvings.
• Enjoy the food stalls: Outside the temple, you'll find numerous food stalls offering snacks and refreshments. Try some delicious vegetarian street food.

A journey of discovery: Visiting Chattarpur Mandir is more than just a sightseeing trip. It's an opportunity to immerse yourself in the rich cultural and spiritual tapestry of India. It's a place where faith, tradition, and modern architecture converge to create an unforgettable experience.","Images":null,"When":""}]

*Review:* Chhatarpur, a lesser-known destination, surprised me with its rich history, cultural significance, and breathtaking natural beauty. The town is home to numerous ancient temples, including the famous Chhatarpur Temple, which showcases stunning architecture and intricate carvings.

The surrounding landscape is dotted with rolling hills, sparkling waterfalls, and dense forests, making it a perfect getaway for nature lovers and adventure seekers. The local cuisine is a delightful mix of traditional flavors and spices, leaving your taste buds craving for more.

The people of Chhatarpur are warm and welcoming, adding to the town's charm. With its unique blend of history, culture, and natural beauty, Chhatarpur is a must-visit destination for anyone looking to explore off-the-beaten-path locations.

*Pros:* Rich history, cultural significance, natural beauty, delicious local cuisine, warm hospitality

*Cons:* Limited infrastructure, requires planning and research to explore fully

*Recommendation:* Chhatarpur is an ideal destination for history buffs, nature lovers, and those seeking an authentic cultural experience. Plan your visit during the monsoon season to witness the town's natural beauty in full bloom!

Established in 1963, this grand temple is dedicated to Goddess Katyayani.

Temple Complex

The entire complex spans over 28 hectares (70 acres), making it one of the largest temple complexes in the country. The temple is nestled amidst beautiful surroundings, offering a serene atmosphere for devotees and visitors.

Ceremonies and Events

During Navratras, various ceremonies such as Hawan, Rudrabhishek, Durga Saptshati Path, Ramayan Path, Rath Puja, and Lungar Puja are performed round-the-clock.

Location

The temple is situated in Dr. Ambedkar Colony, Chhattarpur, Delhi, with a distance of approximately 17.68 kilometers from the city center.
Nearest Metro - Chhatarpur Mandir

Address

Shri Adhya Katyani Shakti Peeth Mandir, Main Chattarpur Road, Chattarpur, Delhi - 110074

Overall, the Chhatarpur Mandir is a significant spiritual destination in Delhi, offering a unique blend of architecture, culture, and devotion.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sAF1QipOiiaukatrW1zRWPmkkLyg2OTtr-PY6vENNc1dT&fid=0x0:0x56e4d17f406c00a8"],"When":"2024-9-8"},{"Name":"Dr. Harshit Lohani","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVg4RIStto7cr2dgUkqhRI1X3uY-F8TYo_H_dNomhfRpEoUDZBM_Q=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Highly spritual place. It was built by the industrialist and philanthropist, Jugal Kishore Birla, and inaugurated by Mahatma Gandhi in 1939. This temple is dedicated to Lord Vishnu, the preserver, and his consort, Goddess Lakshmi, the goddess of wealth and prosperity.

The Laxminarayan Temple is renowned for its stunning architecture and intricate carvings. It features a blend of both traditional and modern architectural styles, making it a unique and visually appealing place of worship. The temple complex includes beautifully landscaped gardens, fountains, and sculptures that add to its aesthetic charm.

Visitors come to the temple not only for its religious significance but also to admire the artistry and serenity it offers. The temple is open to people of all faiths and is a peaceful oasis in the heart of Delhi, providing a tranquil space for meditation and prayer. It's a must-visit destination for both tourists and locals interested in exploring Delhi's cultural and architectural heritage.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Vinita prajapati","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIS5MU6WTUKPSLqrOEtcYlXWSdWDq-b_acWQMac3ldsovtYyA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Very beautiful mandir 🙏","Images":null,"When":""}]

[{"Name":"Avanish Gupta","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVkWHvsGrZy_i7gZWqSke1IRAPV9wJjY3GPbQudVIH1EGNAaoZp=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"In the bustling city of Delhi, the Hanuman Temple in Chhattarpur stands as a serene and sacred haven, dedicated to the revered Hindu deity, Lord Hanuman. Located on Main Chhatarpur Road, this magnificent temple complex is a must-visit destination for spiritual seekers, architecture enthusiasts, and anyone seeking solace in the midst of chaos.

Architecture and Ambiance:
The temple's stunning architecture is a blend of traditional and modern styles, with intricate carvings, ornate pillars, and sprawling courtyards. The complex is spread over 2 acres, with beautifully manicured lawns and tranquil water features, creating a peaceful ambiance that soothes the soul. The main temple is adorned with a majestic 108-foot-tall statue of Lord Hanuman, visible from afar, making it a striking landmark in the area.

Spiritual Significance:
The Hanuman Temple is considered one of the most sacred temples in Delhi, attracting devotees from far and wide. The temple's priests perform regular pujas and rituals, filling the air with sacred chants and prayers. The temple's energy is palpable, making it an ideal spot for meditation, prayer, and spiritual growth.

Facilities and Maintenance:
The temple complex is immaculately maintained, with clean pathways, well-manicured gardens, and adequate seating areas. The management provides various facilities, including:

- Free parking
- Prasad distribution
- Medical facilities
- Shoe racks
- Drinking water
- Restrooms

Cultural Events and Festivals:
The temple hosts various cultural events and festivals throughout the year, such as:

- Hanuman Jayanti celebrations
- Navratri festivities
- Diwali puja
- Ram Navami celebrations

These events showcase the rich cultural heritage of India, attracting visitors from all walks of life.

Conclusion:

The Hanuman Temple in Chhattarpur is a spiritual oasis in the heart of Delhi, offering a serene and sacred space for devotees and visitors alike. Its stunning architecture, tranquil ambiance, and spiritual significance make it a must-visit destination for anyone seeking solace, peace, and spiritual growth. Whether you're a local or a tourist, this temple complex is a treasure trove of Indian culture and spirituality, waiting to be explored and experienced.

Rating: 5/5

Recommendation:

The Hanuman Temple is highly recommended for:

- Spiritual seekers
- Architecture enthusiasts
- Cultural enthusiasts
- Families and friend groups
- Anyone seeking peace and solace in Delhi
.
.
.
Here are some additional aspects that make the Hanuman Temple in Chhattarpur a special destination:

- Temple Museum: The temple complex houses a museum showcasing Lord Hanuman's life and legacy through sculptures, paintings, and artifacts.

- Library: A well-stocked library with religious texts, scriptures, and books on Hindu philosophy and culture.

- Yoga and Meditation: Regular yoga and meditation classes are conducted in the temple premises, promoting physical and mental well-being.

- Food: The temple's prasad (offering) includes delicious vegetarian dishes, distributed free of cost to devotees.

- Cultural Programs: The temple hosts cultural programs, such as music concerts, dance performances, and theater productions, promoting Indian art and culture.

- Community Service: The temple is involved in various community service initiatives, including education, healthcare, and social welfare programs.

- Gift Shop: A well-stocked gift shop selling religious items, souvenirs, and spiritual books.

- Parking and Transport: Ample parking space and easy transportation options, including buses and autos, are available.

- Security: The temple complex has adequate security measures in place, ensuring a safe and secure environment for visitors.

It would easily take u 30 minutes to max an hour to cover the entire temple. Hats off to the workers and engineers and architects who built this. Open from 9am-9pm. The best time to visit is early morning or between 3pm-5pm for less crowds and the position of the sun is good for better pics and videos. #OmNamahShivay 🙏

It would easily take at least 2 hours for you to cover all the 4 places:

1. Chhattarpur Temple
2. Shri Adhya Katyayani Shaktipith Mandir

Known for its massive Hanuman statue and intricate architecture blending North and South Indian styles, the temple attracts numerous devotees and tourists. The complex also includes shrines dedicated to Goddess Katyayani, Lord Shiva, and other deities.

Visitors appreciate the serene atmosphere, the grand aarti ceremonies, and the beautifully maintained gardens. The temple is also known for its charitable initiatives, including schools and medical services for the underprivileged.

It is easily accessible via the Delhi Metro (Chhatarpur station, Gate 2) or by cab. In my opinion either it's a private property or this land is too old to have this mandir created. The mandir is just a below average construction around a old people 🌲 tree. It's an unorganised construction, as an when constructed. Some parts which are new and made of marble look good. The shivling is good. Apart from this mandir consists of many other idols of god and goddess as usually happens in any other mandir. This mandir may be popular among the surrounding area or nearby residents, I don't think most people other than that even know about this mandir. It's a small hindu mandir, ok so some how vibrations were good and atmosphere pleasant. I found this mandir accidentally, It happened that i forgot a street and entered this one and got this mandir. The very kind Panditji or the person incharge lived there inside the same premises of mandir and offered me food. I was overwhelmed by his kind gesture.  So one can visit this mandir, not especially but out of curiosity may be. A must-visit for a spiritual and cultural journey♥️","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sAF1QipPYrQlLvqrdA04TULnDK3srGpSYA-e9Za5-dv7w\u0026fid=0x0:0xd7e35b923b902dcf"],"When":"2024-8-2"},{"Name":"Amit K Khurana (A K K)","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUGjDGQDxa5oYjys14ThA2eXoHSxwSHHxlClU_eVNl4-XhgPo8=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"So this mandir is in Rajpur khurd, chattarpur. It's inside these residential streets. So it's on a private property which was meant for making house but the owner decided to donate it to mandir. Now it's for everyone. Every one is free to visit this mandir. I won't say it's a very big mandir but still even this much area in Delhi is more than big enough. It doesn't look small from any angle. This mandir is famous among those residents who live nearby. It's a great landmark though. People do have great faith in this mandir and there is a rush on several days and at aarti time. Extremely neat and clean, the construction looks very new though it's fifteen years old. Most parts of the mandir is very elegant with marble all over. The idols of god and goddess are very beautiful. The vibrations were excellent and atmosphere very pleasant. Some pictures and videos for your instant view. Panditji takes good care of this place.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sAF1QipPavFWzrRpu3rm3QcTYjC00UcFbfzrfZ8oJThHn\u0026fid=0x0:0xd7e35b923b902dcf"],"When":"2023-3-8"},{"Name":"Heena Kapoor","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX46Z-1kY7r3A0xzSyjk8ttObHkzYaqMHVm6rDVmJZBUIt2FnODMw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"How can someone rate Mandir?\n\nIt's a peaceful place in a nice residential colony","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Dev Kaushik","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJ18L_dJ9YGPMjc9OWR7UPNPa0g3y4x8QC89VnjH_jqdEnFc0o=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"🙏","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sAF1QipOcfBuwSZ_8nTj0FKnxqoRDQTFz-n1mh-Pgc_qz\u0026fid=0x0:0xd7e35b923b902dcf"],"When":"2024-9-5"},{"Name":"Viraj","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWGJlac6x6WkTvgq8vMVNmmZyPa2wdU0G6JWwOssHZTGbaxuqh6=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This salon is a hidden gem!
